title: [Bug]: Parallel MkDocs checks can collide in the shared site directory
summary: Documentation validation can fail when two MkDocs builds run at the same time against the default shared output directory.
observed: A concurrent documentation check can make one MkDocs process clean the shared output directory while another process is still traversing that directory.
expected: Release, CI, and local documentation checks should use isolated output directories so overlapping documentation builds cannot delete each other's files.
reproduction: Run two documentation builds at the same time against the default output directory, or run the focused regression test that verifies release automation uses isolated site directories.
impact: A release engineer can see a false documentation-build failure during release preparation even though the documentation content is valid.
delivery_semantics: The fix affects release validation only and does not change NATS delivery behavior, sink writes, or ACK ordering.
security: The fix keeps generated documentation output in ignored temporary directories and does not expose secrets or live environment details.
acceptance: Release and CI documentation checks use a collision-safe docs wrapper.
The docs wrapper builds Read the Docs and GitHub Pages variants into isolated temporary directories.
The GitHub Pages publishing workflow still produces a single uploadable site artifact.
Focused regression tests and documentation checks pass.
tests: Run the focused docs-build isolation test, bug report validation, documentation checks, and the full local check script before release.
documentation: Update development, release, publishing, Read the Docs, GitHub Pages, README, changelog, and the latest test report.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the collision-safe docs build wrapper is published with regression and verification evidence.
